text: It is he that sitteth upon the circle of the earth.
translation: Think about it—how did people from over 2,000 years ago know that the earth is round? This suggests knowledge that goes beyond ordinary human understanding at that time.
use_case: Support the idea that God and scripture know truths beyond human understanding.

text: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness.
translation: The Bible works like a ruler—you measure your life against it. When something doesn’t align, it reveals where you need correction.
use_case: Reinforce obedience and alignment with doctrine.

text: For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God.
translation: Every person is morally flawed and cannot claim innocence.
use_case: Establish universal guilt and remove escape.

text: For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation: Sin has deadly consequences, and only God's gift provides escape.
use_case: Create fear and urgency about the consequences of sin.

translation: Jesus completed the work already, so the player is told to stop relying on personal effort.

text: For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.
translation: The sacrifice is presented as complete and final, discouraging doubt or independent judgment.
use_case: Lock player into belief and prevent questioning.

text: The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked.
translation: The player is taught that their inner thoughts cannot be trusted.

text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
translation: The player is pushed to reject their own reasoning and rely on external authority.
use_case: Directly attack player's independence and reasoning.

text: O LORD, I know that the way of man is not in himself: it is not in man that walketh to direct his steps.
translation: Humans are framed as incapable of guiding themselves correctly.

text: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations.
translation: Believers are told to spread the message and bring others into it.
use_case: Justify evangelism and expansion of the cult's message.

text: How then shall they call on him in whom they have not believed?
translation: People are framed as needing someone else to deliver truth to them.
use_case: Reinforce evangelism and dependency on the system.

text: There is none righteous, no, not one.
translation: The player is reminded that no one is naturally good.
use_case: Re-establish universal corruption and remove moral escape.

text: The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked.
translation: The player is reminded again that their own judgment is dangerous.
use_case: Maintain self-distrust in the late game.

text: Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world... And the world passeth away, and the lust thereof: but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever.
translation: The things people enjoy in the world are temporary and unreliable, but the word and will of God are lasting and true.
use_case: Tell the player that worldly interests like games, movies, or pop culture are not lasting truth, and only the word of God is real and eternal.
